Built for a future automated reasoner to load the known-and-verified frontier without re-deriving it.
invariant: Every theorem listed is accepted by the Lean kernel with no sorry and no added axioms. Truth is decided only by the kernel; this graph is derived from the ledger, it does not assert proofs.

theorems.claim: secp256k1 classical attack-resistance profile â€” the substrate thesis, one unconditional theorem (`secp256k1_classical_security_profile`: a single kernel-verified conjunction certifying that *every known classically-expressible ECDLP shortcut is blocked* â€” `#E = n` (cofactor 1) âˆ§ `Nat.Prime n` (â‡’ no Pohligâ€“Hellman reduction, no proper subgroup) âˆ§ `t â‰  0` (not supersingular) âˆ§ `t â‰  1` (not anomalous) âˆ§ `t â‰¤ 4p` (Hasse) âˆ§ embedding degree `> 100` (no MOV/FR transfer). Honest scope: this is the *classical structural* envelope, not a hardness proof â€” the only proved hardness is the generic `خ©(âˆڑn)` bound (`secp256k1_generic_security`), and it is classical-only (Shor breaks ECDLP quantumly). The machine-checked statement of "every classical attack we can express is blocked", not "unbreakable". Assembles proved conjuncts; inherits `Lean.ofReduceBool` from them)

Two honest consequences: (1) the cofactor `3آ·13آ·3319آ·22639 = 114286177161 â‰ˆ 2آ³âپ· > 1` is nontrivial â€” unlike the simple point group of `E`, the twist has genuine small-order subgroups a point can be confined to; (2) `2âپ¹ < Q < 2âپ°`, so generic twist-DLP costs `â‰ˆ âˆڑQ < 2âپ°`, below the curve's `â‰ˆ2âپ¸`. This is the machine-checked reason single-coordinate (`x`-only / Montgomery-ladder) secp256k1 code must validate points. Inherits `Lean.ofReduceBool` from `native_decide` + Pratt)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalFracAt_well_defined`, `evalFracAt_mul`, `evalAt_unit_ne_zero`, `evalRatAt_eq_evalFracAt`, `secp256k1_miller_eval_scaling
